situation: A customer asks whether a promising energy-saving measure is really worth pursuing, but the available analysis is incomplete. How do you help the team make progress without overstating what is known?
The project team keeps focusing on incentive dollars, while a larger operational benefit may matter more to the owner. How do you redirect the conversation?
Two members of the design team want different solutions and both look to you for a recommendation. What do you ask before guiding them toward a decision?
A results meeting is running out of time and the customer has not selected a direction. How do you close the meeting with useful momentum?
Required owner information is still missing and the project schedule is beginning to tighten. How do you move the request forward without making the process feel bureaucratic?
A consultant and customer each believe the other person is responsible for the next administrative step. How do you clarify ownership and timing?
A program requirement surprises the project team late in the process. How do you explain it while protecting confidence in the program and the consulting team?
Several project records contain conflicting contact and incentive-recipient information. What should you verify, and how should you communicate the correction?
The installed equipment does not exactly match the assumptions used during design. How do you determine whether the difference matters and explain the next step?
Verification is complete for the current phase, but another team must now lead the next step. How do you communicate the transition while maintaining shared ownership?
A customer questions why additional documentation is necessary when the equipment is visibly installed. How do you explain the value of verification?
Field conditions make one measurement unreliable. How do you document the limitation and still provide the most useful conclusion possible?
The preferred meeting time works for the consultant but excludes a key decision-maker. How do you balance speed with the need for the right people in the room?
A meeting has been rescheduled twice and the project is losing momentum. What message would help secure a firm date without sounding inflexible?
The team asks for a results meeting before the analysis will be ready. How do you clarify expectations and propose a workable alternative?
Several attendees accept the invitation, but no one confirms who has authority to make the needed decision. What should you do before the meeting?
A calculation error appears only sometimes, and no one can reproduce it consistently. What information should you request before trying to fix the bug?
A requested fix could solve one user's problem but create unexpected effects elsewhere. How do you evaluate the trade-off and communicate the risk?
Testing is taking longer than coding because the tool has many edge cases. How do you explain why the QA work is necessary?
A tool works well for small projects but slows dramatically with a very large dataset. How do you determine whether to optimize, redesign, or set a practical limit?
